festivals.name: Paro Tsechu
festivals.description: Bhutan's most famous tsechu festival features masked dances performed by monks at the dzong, culminating in the Thongdrel's unveiling.

festivals.name: Thimphu Tsechu
festivals.description: Bhutan's capital hosts its annual tsechu with three days of sacred mask dances and cultural performances.

destinations.name: Taktsang (Tiger's Nest)
destinations.description: Bhutan's most iconic site: a Buddhist monastery clinging to a granite cliff 900m above the Paro valley floor.

destinations.name: Thimphu
destinations.description: The world's only capital with no traffic lights, Thimphu has a majestic dzong, traditional archery fields, and the Tashichho Dzong.

destinations.name: Punakha Dzong & Valley
destinations.description: Bhutan's most beautiful dzong stands at the confluence of two rivers, surrounded by rice fields and jacaranda trees.

destinations.name: Bumthang Valley
destinations.description: Bhutan's spiritual heartland has some of the oldest temples in the country, including the 7th-century Jambay Lhakhang.

destinations.name: Haa Valley
destinations.description: One of Bhutan's least-visited valleys with pristine forests, traditional villages, and the high Chelela pass.

destinations.name: Jigme Dorji National Park
destinations.description: Bhutan's largest park shelters snow leopards, takins, and blue sheep in pristine Himalayan wilderness.

destinations.name: Wangdue Phodrang
destinations.description: A historic town and dzong perched on a ridge above the Punakha valley, gateway to central Bhutan's remote areas.

destinations.name: Druk Path Trek
destinations.description: The classic 5-day trek connects Paro to Thimphu through alpine lakes, bamboo forests, and stunning Himalayan views.
